offer.what: Residential real estate representation — buyer's agent and listing (seller) services in the Atlanta metro market, operated by Aaron Moen at Keller Williams Realty Intown Atlanta
offer.coreOutcome: A calm, transparent home purchase or sale with a trusted local agent who stays in your corner long after closing
offer.pricePoint: Commission-based; no fee quoted upfront (standard Atlanta market: ~2.5–3% buyer-side, ~5–6% total on listings); ticket size typically $300k–$600k+ per transaction
offer.commitment: Per-transaction (project-based), cultivated for repeat and referral
leadMagnets: Free buyer or listing consultation (Calendly booking)

audience.icp: Atlanta-area first-time homebuyers and current homeowners looking to sell or upsize — value relationship and plain-English guidance over speed or corporate polish
painPoints: Confused by the buying or selling process
Fear of making a costly mistake without a trusted advisor
Distrust of agents who disappear after closing
Unsure whether now is the right time to buy or sell

salesCycle.length: Weeks to months (home search typically 30–90 days; contract-to-close 30–60 days)
salesCycle.trial: Free buyer or listing consultation via Calendly — no commitment, no cost
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Booked call/consultation leading to Buyer Representation Agreement or Listing Agreement
salesCycle.topObjection: Not ready yet / want to shop around / uncertain about market timing

ahaMoment: The first consultation where the client feels heard and the process is demystified — or the first home tour where the stress visibly dissolves

retention.recurring: Not inherently recurring; relationship cultivated for repeat transactions (move-up, investment, etc.) and referrals
retention.expansion: Referrals to family and friends, second transactions (upsizing/downsizing), investment property
retention.churnRisk: Long inter-transaction gap (3–7 years) during which clients forget the agent and accept a referral elsewhere

lifecycleGaps: No apparent automated Welcome & Onboarding sequence for new leads
Lead-magnet questionnaires likely not followed up with nurture email sequence
No post-close stay-in-touch or anniversary campaign
Blog exists but no evidence of email newsletter or value content campaign
No win-back sequence for cold leads or past clients
toneVoice: Warm, casual, southern-hospitable; food-and-home metaphors ('Getting Cooking,' 'The Ingredients'); conversational and personal — avoids corporate real estate jargon; friendly without being sappy
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / email opt-in required; Georgia real estate license disclosures in marketing; no HIPAA or GDPR-specific constraints
